description_long: This object is a large, circular drum enclosure with a beige and white color scheme, featuring a continuous, curved shell supported by vertical black posts at regular intervals. The drum has a hollow center and a raised, flat top surface, suggesting it is designed for use as a performance or display drum in a public or urban setting. Adjacent to the main drum body is a separate, smaller round drum pad mounted on a slender, metallic tripod stand, which appears to be a snare or auxiliary drum. The main drum's exterior panels are smooth and likely made of a composite or synthetic material, bordered by white structural elements. The overall construction is robust, with the main drum forming a partial enclosure or barrier, possibly for crowd control or interactive musical installations. There are no visible handles, lids, or signage, and the surfaces appear clean and well-maintained.
description: A large, curved beige and white drum enclosure with a hollow center and a separate round drum pad on a tripod stand. The structure is smooth, sturdy, and designed for public or urban use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the curved drum enclosure with its hollow center and the separate round drum pad on a tripod stand to the right.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the connection between the main drum body and the adjacent drum pad, with more detail on the enclosure's curvature.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the continuous curve of the drum enclosure and the drum pad positioned to the left.
description_view_3: Side view emphasizing the open center of the drum enclosure and the spatial relationship between the main drum and the tripod-mounted drum pad.
